programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Aaron
programme.desc: On January 31, 2011, 14-year-old Aaron shot his neighbor, 14-year-old Alana, in her home in Harlem, Georgia. Aaron was sentenced to life without parole at 15, but now 21-year-old Aaron is appealing his sentence.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Curtis
programme.desc: On April 10, 1995, in Aurora, Colorado, 15-year-old Curtis was one of four teens involved in the death of Christopher as they attempted to steal his car. After 23 years in jail, Curtis has been granted a resentencing and could soon be released.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: James
programme.desc: On November 24, 1990, 15-year-old James killed his stepmother and three young brothers with a shotgun, and then set their rural Wyoming home on fire.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Bobby
programme.desc: Bobby shot girlfriend Krissi's mother and father as they slept in their home in Frisco, Texas. The mother died, but the father survived. Bobby was sentenced to life despite claims he was manipulated into committing the murder.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Ronald
programme.desc: On February 2, 1999, 17-year-old Ronald and two other teens sought to retaliate against Cordell for an alleged sexual assault ultimately burning him alive in the woods of Fort Walton Beach, Florida.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Preston
programme.desc: On March 4, 2010, 17-year-old Preston was one of three teens involved in a murder during an attempted robbery in Fort Walton Beach, Florida. Along with the other teens, Preston was sentenced to life without parole.

programme.title: Kids Behind Bars: Life or Parole: Otis
programme.desc: On March 31, 1998 in Albany, Georgia, 16-year-old Otis broke into Valerie's home and allegedly tried to rob her. During the attempt, Valerie and her 16-year-old daughter, Valene, were shot. Valerie dies weeks later and her daughter was disfigured.

programme.title: Born Behind Bars: They Can Take Your Baby Away
programme.desc: A controversial prison nursery program at the Indiana women's prison allows a few select inmates to raise their babies behind bars. The rules are strict, there's zero tolerance and one slip can result in an inmate losing her child.

programme.title: Born Behind Bars: Drugs Is The Devil
programme.desc: Stephanie hopes for the early release that will enable her to bring her daughter home even though she hasn't completed her drug abuse treatment. Then, Ruthanne desperately wants to be reunited with her kids.

programme.title: Born Behind Bars: I Don't Want to See This Place Ever Again
programme.desc: Stephanie goes to court, hoping for the early release that will let her bring baby home. An inmate nanny risks getting kicked out the Wee Ones program because of her girlfriend. Taylor tries to get prison officials to let her premature babies join her.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Deal With the Devils
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr invade a silent library to see who can make the most noise without getting into trouble. Plus, they search for their missing wives at the mall, and tonight's two losers get iced out by the New Jersey Devils.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Takes the Cake
programme.desc: Find out if any of the Jokers are avid skiers, and how the maître d' helped when seating patrons. Also, learn why Joe had a hard time escaping the giant cake.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Drum and Drummer
programme.desc: So many shoppers in the candy aisle during 'Star Struck'. The celeb that was on set while the guys filmed 'Brain Games'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Field of Screams
programme.desc: How the creepy voice changer was made for Devil Voices. The penguin tips Murr learned as a zookeeper.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Three Men and Your Baby
programme.desc: The styles of dance Murr claims to be skilled in. The Joker that recently became a published author.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Damned If You Do
programme.desc: Find out which joker once included inline skating as a skill on his resume, who was a mathlete and a bowler in high school and which joker was afraid of the dark as a child.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Washed Up
programme.desc: How the phrase “rooty toot toot” became popular on set, which guy has recently cut his potato intake in half, and how Joe managed to cool off during his sweltering bikini car wash punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: To Hatch a Predator
programme.desc: A last-minute prop request that caused a scramble, where Murr’s fetching housecoat came from, and who was offered a job at the children’s museum following Q’s turtle punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Like a Boss
programme.desc: Why the slow-moving line at The Fast and the Furious ride made things harder for the guys, who officiated Joe's wedding, and why last-minute changes were needed for Murr's Tumblr punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hump Day
programme.desc: Learn how the crew attracted more customers to eat at GRK, what piece of iconic 'Jokers' history was made in this same focus group space and who caused several delays in the filming of Q's zoo punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Crash Test Dummies
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q take some customers for a wild ride, and things get messy when they ask fellow shoppers for a hand. Plus, the big losers suffer a monster punishment that leaves them crushed.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Sal, Murr, Joe and Q pull desk duty, and then do strangers a solid by barring them from the bathroom. Plus, the big loser learns the hard way that gratuity is not included.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Indecent Proposal
programme.desc: Sal, Murr, Joe and Q don absurd disguises to trick unsuspecting receptionists, then try to convince strangers to sign off on some unusual emails. Plus, the big loser turns a harmless dance class into a dance with danger.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Sal, Joe, Murr and Q take a spin in a motorized chair hoping the kindness of strangers will keep them on track. Tonight's big loser walks away bruised and battered when he's forced to clear some tables in an unusual way.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Farm Brunch Anniversary
programme.desc: Molly Yeh is making a special brunch for the family to celebrate her and her husband Nick's third wedding anniversary. There's shakshuka with feta, Brussel sprout and bacon hash, roasted potatoes with paprika mayo and pocket pastries for dessert.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Curling Club
programme.desc: Molly Yeh makes her most-prized Midwestern comfort foods with a twist. The hearty dishes include knoephla soup with the chewiest dumplings ever, sticky ribs and a side of sweet, tangy broccoli slaw with plump currants.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Pizza Friday
programme.desc: It's Pizza Friday on the farm, and Molly Yeh and her husband, Nick, are hosting friends for dinner. Molly's making two pizzas using the same delicious dough recipe: sausage and kale pizza and squash and ricotta pizza with fresh squash from the farm.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Appetizer Perfection
programme.desc: Molly is sharing her favourite appetizer recipes, starting with BBQ pulled pork nachos. She also makes gooey fried mozzarella sticks, ham and cheese jalapeno poppers with a spicy kick and flaky walleye cakes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Make Hay While the Sun Shines
programme.desc: Ree prepares food as the family works around the clock during hay season.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Freezer!
programme.desc: Ree is stocking the freezer with breakfast, lunch, dinner and dessert. To start, there are crunchy French toast sticks followed by Tex Mex butternut squash soup for lunch and casserole royalty with king ranch chicken. For dessert are confetti cups.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Grilling to Go
programme.desc: Ree Drummond's grilling up a well-deserved tailgate lunch. On the menu are smoky salt and pepper drumsticks, flame-grilled cheesy steakburgers, grilled taters with onions and garlic, spicy street corn and blueberry hand pies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Moving House
programme.desc: There's a new cowboy on the ranch, and Ree Drummond is making him dinner to-go and sharing the epic story of delivering his massive, ready-built house.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Sweet Talk
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is celebrating the opening of her new sweet shop with a lineup of treats and sundaes. She makes incredible, old-fashioned Jeweled Divinity and shares the secrets to two of the over-the-top sundaes she serves at the parlor.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Pizza Ree-A
programme.desc: Ree Drummond's sharing recipes from her pizza place in town. For takeout with a difference, she makes foolproof pizza dough, garlic butter and a no-cook pizza sauce and uses them for her triple pepperoni pizza and her signature pie, pizza Ree-a.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: All-Seafood Battle
programme.desc: Four seafood specialists battle it out in Flavortown. Guy Fieri limits the chefs to just ten ingredients in the first round, and then all the ingredients for their second dish must start with the same random letter.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Fried Feud
programme.desc: It's nothing but fried fun when four chefs are challenged with making dishes full of fried fair food. They will have to follow a bizarre grocery list for the first challenge before getting a skimpy budget to make fried chicken for the next challenge.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Battle America!
programme.desc: Chefs from four distinct American regions vie for culinary supremacy. First up, turning a hometown hit into a homerun when you only get five ingredients. Next, it's sink or swim when chefs make a seafood supper using nothing but frozen food.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: A Dicey Situation
programme.desc: Four chefs take a gamble when Guy makes them roll dice to determine the details for creating their best dish. Next, the chefs must use odds and ends from the Clearance Carts to make a classy champagne brunch.

programme.title: Ice Cold Gold: Midnight Run
programme.desc: The miners begin to journey into the unexplored and unforgiving wilderness of Greenland. While searching for the ultimate treasure, half the team ends up stranded five miles from camp while the rest discover what could be a massive gold deposit.

programme.title: Ice Cold Gold: Death From Above
programme.desc: The miners split into two groups and head out on a recon mission going on almost no sleep. One group hits a deadly valley that has massive boulders coming down.

programme.title: Ice Cold Gold: Frozen with Fear
programme.desc: Eric pushes his team to explore every inch of the mountains around their camp as time gets shorter in Saqqaq. Americo and Eric trek up a 1200 foot slope in search of gold when Jesse and Gator refuse.

programme.title: Bering Sea Gold: Double Down
programme.desc: Despite the worst conditions in a decade, the miners hedge their bets as Shawn and Ken go head to head with their largest ships ever. Emily confronts the elements while Kris leads his family with a newfound determination.

programme.title: Bering Sea Gold: Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: Shawn's rival Ken unleashes his 600-ton gold gobbler while Shawn fights to keep a family dynasty. Vernon makes a valiant return in an attempt to strike it rich with a new dredge. Emily fights to reclaims her hotspot.

programme.title: Bering Sea Gold: Clash of the Titans
programme.desc: An epic Clash of Titans grips the gold mining fleet as Shawn constructs the largest washplant in Nome and Ken's mining monster roars to life. Vern prospects using his new innovations. Ripping currents threaten the Kellys.

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters: Rookie Mistakes
programme.desc: A new season opal hunting begins, and the Rookies face potential disaster from the get go. Col's Crew's mining quest is derailed by a serious illness, and the Opal Queen brings in new muscle and new machines for an underground opal hunt.

programme.title: Wolves and Warriors: Welcome to the Wolf Sanctuary
programme.desc: Matt and Lorin run a sanctuary for rescued wolves and struggling combat veterans. Matt and the team rescue Sadira, a grey wolf out of a backyard in Oregon, while US Army veteran Albert joins the team as he tries to mend his relationship with his family.

programme.title: Wolves and Warriors: A Wolf Romance
programme.desc: When a nearby animal sanctuary with over 400 animals is threatened by a California wildfire, the LARC team races into the flames to assist in the evacuation. A tumor is discovered on LARC's elder statesmen, an almost 20-year old wolf named Blade.

programme.title: Wolves and Warriors: The Heart of a Wolf
programme.desc: Matt and the veterans are called in when a neglected and aggressive wolfdog named Apache needs to be removed from the rough conditions of a suburban neighborhood home. Apache quickly finds comfort at LARC, and the love and companionship of a female wolf.

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Bear Naked Truth

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Old Men and The Lake

programme.title: Northwest Law: Of Clams and Car Crashes

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Man with No Name
programme.desc: The Fish and Wildlife officers encounter a slew of difficult and dangerous suspects including a man refusing to reveal his identity.

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Weedwhackers
programme.desc: The officers carefully approach a suspect with a machete who is suspected of running an illegal marijuana farm while Sgt. Jewell stumbles upon a young drifter on Moses Lake.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Outfoxed

programme.desc: Alison and Donovan take a huge risk when they buy an oddball, boxlike house from the 90s. A flooded basement is just the beginning of the problems they encounter, so Alison must find a way to bring big design to a house that's hurtling over budget.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Aloha Florida
programme.desc: A woman follows a job from Hawaii to Florida, but it comes with one massive challenge: she has to start in two weeks. She and her husband have one weekend to find the perfect home for their multi-generational family.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Big Choices in San Antonio
programme.desc: A man learns he must report for duty in six weeks, not six months, pushing him and his wife to quickly settle on a house in San Antonio. They find two good options, but the house hunt comes to a halt when she can't decide what's best for the family.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Charlotte Scramble
programme.desc: A couple gets a huge opportunity when his job promotion moves them to Charlotte, NC, but that means leaving her parents and sister behind. With a toddler and an incoming high schooler, they must find a home before school starts.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Hot Market in Atlanta
programme.desc: Jenn and Ryan have already relocated several times for work, but with their two children now in high school, a move from Seattle to Atlanta could be the hardest one yet. With Ryan on the road more than at home, they have just two days to find a home.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Taking Chances in Tucson
programme.desc: With just weeks left to move out of their house in California, a couple are under intense pressure to find a new home in Tucson, Arizona. They couple has three children, including two with special needs, making for a very specific wish list.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Independence in Indiana
programme.desc: A couple jumps at the opportunity to move their blended family closer to kin in Indianapolis. However, they only have four weeks to move, leaving them with one weekend to find their next home.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Financial Freedom in Fort Worth
programme.desc: A woman gets to live her dream of being a stay-at-home mom when her husband lands a new job in Fort Worth, TX. However, he must start his new position immediately and they are in a rush to find their forever home.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Home for the Holidays
programme.desc: A couple wants to relocate from Minnesota to New Jersey to be closer to family. Her goal is to be in their new home in time to have Thanksgiving with loved ones, but they struggle to find a home that checks off their wish list.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Putting Down Roots in Georgia
programme.desc: A military family that's used to moving with little notice receives his latest post orders. They hope to find a forever home in Georgia where they can settle down when he retires in six years.

programme.title: House in a Hurry: Empty Nesting in Tampa
programme.desc: A couple was about to become empty nesters before he accepted a high-profile CEO position that will move them from Denver to Tampa. They have just three days to find a house that's perfect for entertaining clients before his position starts in six weeks.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: More Is More
programme.desc: This family has lived in their home for three years now and while the kids love the backyard pool, mom and dad have been struggling with a poorly designed kitchen and a basement that's been a DIY disaster.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Growing Boys, Grander House
programme.desc: Jonathan steps in to overhaul tired interiors, determined to bring this 1950s home into the new millennium. The owners' firefighter buddies pitch in during demo, and then it's full steam ahead- once Drew can expand the buyers' location horizons.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Starter Home to Forever Home
programme.desc: Sheryl and Frank's suburban neighbourhood was a welcome change when they were looking to grow their young family, but with two soon-to-be teenagers, they're ready to find their forever home. The Property Brothers are determined to make it happen.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: No Horsing Around
programme.desc: A family tried rural living for a few years but soon realized maintaining a 10-acre property is a full-time job. Now they're on the hunt for a turnkey property that's closer to friends and work.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: Finally Finished Family Home
programme.desc: A couple ripped apart their home for an all-out overhaul, but a new job stalled the project and now they're stuck in a DIY disaster. Jonathan steps in to finish the job, however, he soon uncovers a host of scary and expensive fixes.

programme.title: Buying and Selling With the Property Brothers: East Vs. West
programme.desc: With a growing family, this couple is desperate to buy into the market while they can.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Swimming With Sharks
programme.desc: It's not all fun in the Tahitian sun as the women of the OC travel to the island of Moorea. When they go swimming with sharks, Meghan discovers that the real danger isn't in the water. Later, Shannon, Vicki, and Tamra take numerous shots at the bar.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Girl Code
programme.desc: Hung-over from a night of drinking, Vicki and Shannon deepen their friendship by discussing the details of David's affair. Meanwhile, the other ladies head out on an excursion that puts them in deep water. Tamra sparks controversy over girl code.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: A Psychic Surprise
programme.desc: Back in the OC, Shannon and David discover that disciplining their kids is easier said than done. Vicki learns that Brooks has made a life altering decision, to stop chemo and try a radical new approach. Meanwhile, Tamra’s psychic makes a shocking claim.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Racing to the Truth
programme.desc: Meghan, Heather, Tamra and Shannon head to NASCAR while Vicki enjoys a birthday getaway with Brooks. Later, Shannon tries to put on a happy face during her birthday dinner with David and her girls.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Sex, Lies and Leeches
programme.desc: Heather’s luncheon ends with a bang as Vicki storms off, upset with Meghan for continually questioning Brooks' cancer treatment. Shannon visits Dr. Moon to help deal with pent-up resentment. Tamra hosts a wild 'sex party.'

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: A Storm Is Coming
programme.desc: The day after Tamra’s sex party, the ladies are still in disbelief over Vicki’s attack on Meghan. Meanwhile, Shannon’s kids cook up a plan to improve their parents' troubled relationship. Tamra and Eddie’s date night turns sour.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Fire Signs
programme.desc: When Shannon throws an Aries-themed party, Brooks finally gets his chance to confront Meghan face-to-face for her relentless digging into his past. Meanwhile, an off-handed comment by Brooks throws Tamra into a rage.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Suspicious Minds
programme.desc: Vicki and Brooks make an early exit from the Aries party leaving the other ladies questioning Brooks' behaviour and Vicki's involvement. Heather and Tony disagree over a design for the new hours, and Tamra shows Ryan his new home in the OC.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Broken Records
programme.desc: As Shannon seeks to make sense of her friendships, Tamra looks to a higher source to improve her son's troubled relationship. A new revelation about Brooks has everyone stunned.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Orange County: Satan Loves Confusion
programme.desc: Vicki's daughter Briana visits the OC and meets Tamra for lunch with some shocking new information about Brooks. The ladies prep for Tamra's baptism in some unconventional ways.

programme.title: Counting Cars: G-T Whoa!
programme.desc: Danny spots a killer 1967 Pontiac GTO at one of the biggest car shows in Vegas. Later, he goes all in on a legendary Harley Davidson Fat Boy and a 1968 Camaro SS 350.

programme.title: Counting Cars: The Horn Ultimatum
programme.desc: Danny discovers the rarest Cadillac collection he's ever seen. Later, Horny Mike has 30 days to move his big ugly bus from the shop's parking lot before Danny has it sent to the dump.

programme.title: Counting Cars: McQueen for a Day
programme.desc: The guys try to take a beat up '68 Mustang Fastback and turn it into Steve McQueen's old "Bullitt" ride within Rick's modest budget.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Firebird Fever
programme.desc: 'Engine Whisperer' Doc has a cool '67 Firebird he's been working on. While he's a mechanical maestro under the hood, his paint skills are lacking so he enlists Ryan to make it look as mean as it will run. Danny checks out a one-of-a-kind car collection.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Ultimate Challenge
programme.desc: Danny takes on his most emotional project yet after spotting a '70 Dodge Challenger on a private road.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Muscle Memory
programme.desc: Kevin is won over by a hotrod pick-up truck. Danny tracks down a customer's original 1969 Firebird, but its terrible condition may make it impossible to restore, and an unannounced visitor causes drama at the shop.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Special Delivery
programme.desc: After blowing two sales, Danny finally gets to customize a 1960 Chevy Delivery the way he wants to. Then, Danny gets some bad news on a client and Mike goes head to horn with 2000 lbs. of angry bull.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Quadzilla
programme.desc: Danny and Kevin visit mad mechanic Gordon to see his latest insane creation. Later, Danny tries to turn a '34 Ford into a hot rod.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Pimpmobile
programme.desc: The shop finally gets to work on Danny's famous '71 Super Fly Cadillac, but Kevin isn't feeling the love. Later, Grandpa wins big at poker, but blows a deal for a new motorcycle.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Danny Takes the Heat
programme.desc: Danny buys a '78 Lincoln with some serious fire damage, but it's only when he takes it apart does he learn how bad it is.

programme.title: Counting Cars: I Want My Caddy Back
programme.desc: Danny gets a call from Kevin's brother, who wants to sell Danny a 1969 Cadillac, but they can't let Kevin know or else he'll never trust his brother or Danny again.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Willys for Warriors
programme.desc: Danny checks out a collection that includes a 1933 Ford Roadster and a '53 Henry J. Meanwhile Kevin, Ryan and Mike make an unauthorised purchase and the pressure is on Ryan to show it's worthwhile.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Burning Out
programme.desc: Danny has big retro plans for a 1972 Cutlass he hopes to flip, and is counting on his fab team to do her right. Roli seeks the help of the guys to learn to do a burn-out. Danny checks out a 1971 Torino whose owner can't seem to put a price on it.

programme.title: Counting Cars: One Sweet 'Stang
programme.desc: Danny scores a 1967 Mustang that he hopes he can turn into another Count's Kustoms masterpiece. He visits a local muscle car collection for inspiration, and later brings in an expert from the Shelby headquarters for a seal of approval.

programme.title: Counting Cars: You Never Forget Your First Z28, Part One

programme.title: Counting Cars: Who's Your Caddy?
programme.desc: Danny acquires a '62 Caddy which he hopes will be a huge flip. Ryan devises a secret plan for the car behind Danny's back, but when Danny finds out, there's some explaining to do. A snafu threatens to turn the Caddy into a money pit.

programme.title: Counting Cars: You Never Forget Your First Z28, Part Two
programme.desc: Danny's battle with nightmare Z28 continues as he puts it through endless fabrication to bring it back to life. When the client comes to pick it up, Danny will finally learn if it was worth all the trouble.

programme.title: Counting Cars: Chrome Is Where the Heart Is

programme.title: Counting Cars: Finders Keepers
programme.desc: Danny acquires a '74 Ford f100 he can't wait to trick out and flip for a big profit. But after the Count falls in love with the truck and decides he wants to keep it, Kevin must sell it before Danny catches on.

programme.desc: Shannon recruits the guys to help him finish up a gift for Danny: a bike he started building for him 10 years ago. Ryan works to keep Danny out of the shop so that Shannon can finish the bike, and winds up buying a sweet Baja Bug in the process.

programme.desc: Astronaut Mike Massismino uncovers the violent secrets of the most deadly planets outside our solar system.

programme.title: The Planets and Beyond: Earth: The Secret History

programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets: Hunt for the Mars Aliens

programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets
programme.desc: Explorers tackle the mysteries of life itself leading us to worlds never before seen.

What on Earth?: Americas' Lost Romans

programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets
programme.desc: Explorers tackle the mysteries of life itself leading us to worlds never before seen.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Universe: Our Solar System
What on Earth?: Americas' Lost Romans
programme.desc: The remains of an abandoned settlement high in the mountains of Peru could be an ancient Roman village, leaving experts to speculate if the Romans visited the Americas centuries before Columbus; if so, it could point to long-lost gold.

programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets: Hunt for the Mars Aliens
programme.desc: NASA's brand-new Mars rover is on the hunt for alien life, and with the help of cutting-edge engineering, scientists embark on a search for fossilized remains.

programme.title: Unearthed: Vesuvius's Secret Victim
programme.desc: Scientists are using cutting edge technology to unearth a second city that, like the well-known city of Pompeii, was destroyed when Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD. This new town is revealed to be one of the most well-preserved Roman towns on earth.

programme.title: Unearthed: Rome's Great Wall
programme.desc: Hadrian's Wall was built to guard the frontiers of the ancient Roman Empire. Now, discoveries reveal who the Romans were trying to keep out with this 72-mile-long megastructure, and what it tells us about the ancient people of Iron Age Britain.

programme.title: Unearthed: Hitler's Lost Bunker
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler's underground bunker was the home of some of the greatest secrets of the Nazi's. Now, experts use cutting-edge tech to discover new evidence of Hitler's evil vision for global domination.

programme.title: Unearthed: Leaning Tower of Pisa: The New Mystery
programme.desc: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is one of the world's most iconic buildings, and it's also the site of an 800-year-old mystery; using cutting-edge tech, experts investigate the archaeological evidence to reveal when it might finally fall over.

programme.title: Unearthed: Ghosts of the Wild West
programme.desc: The gunfight at Tombstone's OK Corral turned Wyatt Earp into an American folk hero, and to find what really happened here, experts use the newest tech to discover what caused the fatal shootout and what it reveals about the dark secrets of the Wild West.

programme.title: Unearthed: Tower of Babel: The New Evidence
programme.desc: The Tower of Babel is one of the most famous stories of the Bible, and to find out if it really existed, archaeologists use cutting-edge tech and the latest science to investigate how ancient engineers could have built such an extraordinary megastructure.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Resurrecting Notre Dame
programme.desc: When catastrophic fires ravaged Notre-Dame in April 2019, one of the world's great wonders was left in ruins. Experts use the latest technology and modern-day science to reveal what it would take to rebuild it a second time.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Rise of the Aqueducts
programme.desc: Rome's aqueducts are one of the greatest engineering marvels in history, and it took dangerous, cutting-edge construction to build them. Experts use the latest tech and modern-day science to reveal if building them a second time would be just as deadly.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Hunt for Noah's Ark
programme.desc: According to the Bible, Noah's Ark was a world-changing engineering feat, and it took cutting-edge construction to build it. Experts use the latest tech and modern-day science to reveal if building it a second time would be just as difficult.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Secrets at the Great Wall
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is one of the world's greatest engineering marvels, and it took dangerous, cutting-edge construction to build it. Experts use the latest tech and modern-day science to reveal if building it a second time would be just as deadly.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Fortress of the Gods
programme.desc: Rome's Pantheon hides the secret to the Roman Empire's power, and it took dangerous, cutting-edge engineering to build it. Experts use the latest tech and modern-day science to reveal if building it a second time would be just as deadly.

programme.title: If We Built It Today: Ghosts of the Colosseum
programme.desc: Rome's Colosseum is the most famous stadium on the planet, and it took cutting-edge engineering and a fallen emperor to build it. Experts use the latest tech and modern-day science to reveal if building it a second time would be just as difficult.

programme.title: Deadly Women: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Some women can't admit their failings. They point the finger of blame at others.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love Turns to Hate
programme.desc: Love is a powerful emotion but so is hate.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Two to Tangle
programme.desc: These deadly women liked company when they killed.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Shattered Love
programme.desc: A pregnant wife murdered, a secret affair and a search for justice spanning twenty years.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Shattered Love
programme.desc: A pregnant wife murdered, a secret affair and a search for justice spanning twenty years.

programme.title: Living a Nightmare: The Good Doctor
programme.desc: A Florida psychiatrist and his son are shot dead, and their home ransacked. Investigators suspect robbery until they realize that only a gun and a car are missing. Now they must work backward to uncover a motive so they can track down a killer.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Mistress of Death
programme.desc: Two women living next to each other since childhood descend into bitter conflict when the ex husband of one starts seeing the other. Add property issues to this toxic mix and the result is an act of shocking violence that engulfs all.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Chance Encounter
programme.desc: A night of partying by fraternity brothers ends with a knock on the door and gunfire. One student is dead, three are wounded, and a freshman freely admits he was the shooter. He claims it was self-defence. But others say he was looking for revenge.

programme.title: Living a Nightmare: The Good Doctor
programme.desc: A Florida psychiatrist and his son are shot dead, and their home ransacked. Investigators suspect robbery until they realize that only a gun and a car are missing. Now they must work backward to uncover a motive so they can track down a killer.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Mountain of Madness
programme.desc: A wealthy executive comes into conflict with his neighbors when he forbids them access to his extensive lands. One local handyman fights to keep the roads and trails open for all, a struggle that ends when one man lies dead.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Searching for Sherri
programme.desc: A young mother vanishes while out jogging. A frantic search is mounted. Then, just as suddenly, three weeks later she reappears, battered and bruised and her long blond hair chopped off.

programme.title: American Nightmare: The Answering Machine
programme.desc: When a charitable, up-and-coming actress is savagely murdered in her home, police believe an angry, revenge-hungry killer is responsible. No one could expect the lies, obsessions, and evidence that will be uncovered in this shocking cold case.

programme.title: American Nightmare: Apartment 23
programme.desc: With graduation nearing at Northeastern University, students are alarmed at the discovery of a fellow co-ed shot to death in her apartment. Detectives face many challenges in the investigation until they realize the answer is right in front of them.

programme.title: I Am Homicide: From the Gridiron to the Grave
programme.desc: High School football star Travis Davis is gunned down on the mean streets of the Queen City, sending detective Garry McFadden on the hunt for justice. McFadden discovers a beef from the street might be the lead that sends him straight to his killer.

programme.title: I Am Homicide: Holy Terror
programme.desc: Charlotte, NC Homicide Detective Garry McFadden finds himself playing a frustrating cat-and-mouse game with a seemingly religious family of church-goers, pastors and a killer.

programme.title: I Am Homicide: The Body in the Rug
programme.desc: Detectives discover that murder may be a family affair, when they discover a body wrapped up in a rug and stashed in the corner of an apartment.

programme.title: I Am Homicide: Philadelphia Freedom
programme.desc: When a nursing student is pulled from her smoldering home, Detective McFadden must confront her lover, ex-boyfriend, and best friend - to learn who lit the match.

programme.title: Nightmare Next Door: The Art of Murder
programme.desc: As 22 year old Kristin Huggins is about to make her artistic debut she suddenly disappears. Soon its clear that the painter from PA had a brush with disaster in nearby NJ. Despite their best efforts, it takes cops quite some time to catch her killer.

programme.title: Nightmare Next Door: Deadly Deception
programme.desc: When 19-year old Danielle Davis is found dead in her college apartment, strangled with the cord from her laptop, Statesboro, Georgia police know they'd better be quick and find this co-ed's killer. When they discover you can't judge a book by its cover.

programme.title: Nightmare Next Door: Lone Star Mystery
programme.desc: The crime rate in rural Royse City, TX is almost non-existent. So when quiet couple Dennis and Norma Woodruff is found murdered in their own home, it sends shock waves through town. As police investigate, it seems the killings...are a family affair.

programme.title: Nightmare Next Door: Everyone Has a Motive
programme.desc: When John Kowalczyk is shot dead, rumors fly in the quiet town of Vienna, VA. Friends can't imagine who would murder the successful entrepreneur. But as police look into John's inner circle, a web of suspects emerges that has the whole town on edge.

programme.title: Your Worst Nightmare: Life on the Line
programme.desc: Cousins Ana Franklin and Jennifer Holliday are traveling home late one night when terror strikes. As Jennifer is taken hostage by an unstable man, she soon connects with a 9-1-1 operator who will stop at nothing to see that Jennifer makes it out alive.

programme.title: Your Worst Nightmare: A Way Out
programme.desc: An investigation into the missing persons case of 13-year-old Jessyca Mullenberg.

programme.title: Your Worst Nightmare: Cooking with Fire
programme.desc: After leaving a violent ex-husband and firing a problem employee, 21-year-old Cathy Whitehead is happy to build a life with the man of her dreams. But when a stranger shows up on her doorstep one night, she's soon caught up in the web of a familiar foe.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Designed for Power
programme.desc: Hitler's mastery of propaganda, and instinct for effective design, used to create everything from the swastika flag to the striking uniforms of the storm troopers, help win huge support for the Nazi party, a vital step in its march towards total power.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: The Industry of War
programme.desc: Hitler is determined to rebuild Germany's military strength after the destruction of almost all its weaponry in the First World War. To overcome his country's dire shortage of raw materials, he plans to steal them from other countries.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Himmler's Empire of Terror
programme.desc: Hitler's bid for power depends on his most loyal, and violent supporters: the SS. Using surveillance, detention, and even murder, they defeat political enemies and are most responsible for putting Hitler's deadly policy of racial purity into practice.

programme.title: Weaponology: Armored Personnel Carriers
programme.desc: Trace the family trees of the greatest fighting vehicles and armoured personnel carriers in history.

programme.title: Weaponology: Bomber
programme.desc: In eight decades man went from the invention of powered flight to the B2 Stealth bomber.

programme.title: Weaponology: Fighter
programme.desc: Exploring the development of engine power, wings, maneuverability and ejection systems, find out how fighter jets now rule the land and sky.

programme.title: Weaponology: Frags, Pineapples, and RPG's
programme.desc: Weaponology traces the history of portable firepower. This family of weapons pack an explosive punch, but can be carried and operated by small infantry units.

programme.title: Weaponology: Bomber
programme.desc: In eight decades man went from the invention of powered flight to the B2 Stealth bomber.

programme.title: Weaponology: Fighter
programme.desc: Exploring the development of engine power, wings, maneuverability and ejection systems, find out how fighter jets now rule the land and sky.

programme.title: Weaponology: Frags, Pineapples, and RPG's
programme.desc: Weaponology traces the history of portable firepower. This family of weapons pack an explosive punch, but can be carried and operated by small infantry units.

programme.desc: Trace the family trees of the greatest fighting vehicles and armoured personnel carriers in history.

programme.desc: Hitler's bid for power depends on his most loyal, and violent supporters: the SS. Using surveillance, detention, and even murder, they defeat political enemies and are most responsible for putting Hitler's deadly policy of racial purity into practice.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: The Industry of War
programme.desc: Hitler is determined to rebuild Germany's military strength after the destruction of almost all its weaponry in the First World War. To overcome his country's dire shortage of raw materials, he plans to steal them from other countries.

programme.title: Inside the Gangster's Code: 18th Street
programme.desc: Lou joins 18th Street gangsters, in prison and on the street, to learn why their deadly rivalry with the MS-13 is devastating El Salvador.

programme.title: Triggers: Weapons That Changed the World: Spec Ops Pistols
programme.desc: For today's elite warriors, their last line of defence is the spec ops pistols. Wil tests the Navy Seal Sig Sauer P226, then fires the greatest forerunners like James Bond's Walther PPK, the Soviet Tokarev and the massive Desert Eagle.

programme.title: Inside the Gangster's Code: The Commandos
programme.desc: Lou Ferrante travels to Manila, in the Philippines, to meet one of the world’s most ruthless prison gangs and find out more about Manila’s streets.

programme.title: Triggers: Weapons That Changed the World: The Abrams Tank
programme.desc: The M1 Abrams tank is the world's most potent armored vehicle. Track its development through such forerunners as the WWII-era M18 Hellcat and the Cold War-era Soviet T-55 tank and see why these 'beast-boxes' have ruled ground warfare for decades.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Saddam: The Butcher of Baghdad
programme.desc: The story of Saddam Hussein, the sadistic tyrant who ruled Iraq with an utter contempt for humanity for 23 years, a man whose cruelty knows no limits and who'll stop at nothing to achieve the power of life and death over millions of people.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Bin Laden: A Terrorist Mastermind
programme.desc: See how Osama bin Laden's journey unfolds from his roots as a privileged young Saudi boy to becoming the formidable leader of al-Qaeda, and his lifetime commitment to violent jihad against the West. This resulted in the horrific 9/11 attacks in 2001.

programme.title: Evolution of Evil: Hitler: The Benchmark of Terror
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler murders six million Jews and invades peaceful countries brutally killing anyone in his way. Find out what could turn a high school dropout into the most evil dictator the world has ever seen.

programme.title: Nazi Secret Files: High Hitler and His Nazi Supersoldiers
programme.desc: Newly discovered secret files reveal the huge drugs program that fuelled the Nazi war machine. Led by Hitler, himself possessed by narcotics, the German troops consume tens of millions of Crystal Meth tablets throughout the war, driving them to extremes.

programme.title: Warrior POV: Tet Offensive
programme.desc: During the infamous Tet Offensive of 1968, an elite band of US Special Operations troops are called in to rescue dozens of helpless civilians trapped in the border town of Chau Doc, which has been captured by an overwhelming Viet Cong invasion force.

programme.title: Warrior POV: Road to Baghdad
programme.desc: See the shot by shot firefights in the 21 day bloody battle for Baghdad from the warrior's point of view.

programme.title: Warrior POV: Search and Destroy
programme.desc: In a battle known as the Nine Days in May, the troops of the US 4th Infantry Division are on a search and destroy mission in Vietnam's Central Highlands when they run headlong into a massive force of North Vietnamese soldiers on the Cambodian border.

programme.title: Gourmet Holiday

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: The Honey Hole
programme.desc: Mark needs a lot of inventory in a hurry so he buys three cabins. It's a fast-paced triple takedown full of suspense, strategy and big crashes. The guys are still able to discover family stories that give these logs history and meaning.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Good Neighbors
programme.desc: Mark and the guys take down a dangerous barn for their new neighbours and build a replica of Abraham Lincoln's boyhood cabin. Graham learns to make bentwood furniture, and the teams visit a stunning opera house.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Tamara's Tudor Tutorial
programme.desc: Tamara Day takes on a 1927 Tudor by blowing out the existing small kitchen and outfitting it with a large island and eating nook. She transforms a bedroom into a dining room, but her hopes of refurbishing the deck begin to break apart.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Shower Showstopper
programme.desc: Tamara takes on a behemoth 1927 Tudor house, but while she admires the home's original details, her dad discovers a problem. She uses a kitchenette to create a bar, revamps an uneven patio and enlarges the master bathroom.

programme.title: Flea Market Flip: Bustin' Up and Bustin' Out
programme.desc: Brooklyn buddies Anthony and Jack go up against cute and crafty lovebirds Cole and Kelly.

programme.title: Flea Market Flip: Bilingual Beauties vs. Flip-Sters
programme.desc: Professional flip-sters, Jen and Kyle go head-to-head with bilingual beauties, Lindsay and Marizol (they might speak Spanish and Creole, but their first language is bargaining).

programme.title: Flea Market Flip: City vs. Country
programme.desc: City slickers Jill and James are ready for anything, including taking on sassy country girls, Karla and Michelle.

programme.title: Flea Market Flip: It's a Flipping Battle Between the Empire State and the Garden State
programme.desc: Team New Jersey, Carolyn and Robert go head-to-head with Team New York, Amanda and Susan. The flipping begins when the teams get $500 and only one hour to find pieces that need a little TLC at Brimfield Antique Show and Flea Market in Brimfield.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: All Bricked Out
programme.desc: Tamara takes an 1892 brick Craftsman that was converted into three apartments and tries to restore it back into a single-family dwelling while keeping original details.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: The Swing of Things
programme.desc: Tamara continues working on an 1892 Craftsman and struggles to add a master bathroom. Then, she finds a way to repurpose floor boards into a porch swing, but her plan to reuse a clawfoot tub hits a snag because of a narrow doorway.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Fireplaces and Wired Spaces
programme.desc: Tamara annexes an oversized dining room to enlarge a tiny kitchen in a 1926 English Tudor. She rips out an outdated and tacky mantel to create a stunning fireplace surround, and then creates a large desktop for an in-home office.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Two-deck Tudor
programme.desc: Tamara struggles to create a more spacious master suite until she decides to add a second deck to a 1926 English Tudor. In the backyard, she adds an outdoor living area with a fire pit. She and her dad then realize the garage doors may cause a problem.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Wine Stave Porch Railing
programme.desc: Bug and his team attempt a large, stubborn tear down that could be their biggest score ever, if the materials are even salvageable. Then, the gang takes on a front porch renovation that forces them to get creative with 18th-century Victorian materials.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Kitchen Calamity
programme.desc: Nicole is hired by one of her clients to rehab his cluttered, compact kitchen into a useable, restored space for his family.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Bathroom Beautification
programme.desc: Nicole takes on another design project while waiting for the Minnehaha house to sell. This time it's her client's colourful sea themed bathroom that needs fixing.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Living Room Overhaul
programme.desc: Continuing her restoration of Adam and Rachael's home, Nicole moves her attention to the living room, where she must restore the beat up hardwood floors.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Tile Terrors
programme.desc: Turning her attention to Adam and Rachael's bathroom, Nicole deals with the many problems that creep up while working with tile in an old house. When she hits a snag, she must figure out a way to make her fixtures fit in the small space.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Garage Door Greatness
programme.desc: With the Minnehaha house still sitting on the market, Nicole decides to put her own house up for sale in hopes that it will sell faster. But before she can do this she gives her garage a much-needed makeover and removes asbestos from her basement.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Unfinished-Finished Basement
programme.desc: Preparing to put her house up for sale, Nicole turns her attention to the cluttered basement and devises a plan to update the space while also keeping the unfinished, industrial feel it would have had when the house was built.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: New Project, New Headache
programme.desc: When a hundred-year-old Victorian home that Nicole wants to renovate sells to other buyers, she convinces them to let her be involved as they work to get it back on the market.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Kitchen Conundrums
programme.desc: Nicole searches for a set of salvaged cabinets to use in the Harriet Project's upper unit kitchen. With her own house still up for sale, Nicole visits the historical society to see if she can discover any new information about the property.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Living Room Revamp
programme.desc: Nicole repairs the hardwood floors in both living rooms of the Harriet Project and searches for replica tile to begin restoring a fireplace in the downstairs unit.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: The Finished Project
programme.desc: With only a few weeks left to finish the Harriet Project, Nicole and the homeowners divide and conquer to complete the lower unit's living room and kitchen. Nicole visits a local high school shop class to work on a radiator cover for the living room.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston: Mystery House
programme.desc: Michael surprises Ashley with an 1893 raised cottage that he purchased before seeing the full interior. The duo soon finds themselves in over their heads when they realize it's not quite the hidden gem Michael thought it was.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston: Dream House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley stumble upon a pre-1900s Victorian after some curbside dumpster diving. They hit New Orleans for some perfect vintage decor then return to combine two rooms into one spacious kitchen and make this the dream house worth of the name.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston: 1913 Beach Bungalow Restoration
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley find a rundown 1913 beach bungalow that's about to be demolished. They transform the small kitchen into a modern, open-concept space with vintage touches and restore the original hardwood floors and shiplap walls.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston: The Kettle House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley get the opportunity to renovate a bizarre Galveston icon that may harbor a few surprises inside. The spaceship-shaped landmark is soon brimming with life as the Cordrays bring their unique charm to the one-of-a-kind house.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Fueling The Fires In Texas
programme.desc: Roger Mooking's quest for lip-smacking smoked meat leads him back to the Lone Star State. In Houston, Texas, Grant Pinkerton is an award-winning pit master who recently opened up his first restaurant.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Where There's Smoke
programme.desc: Roger Mooking follows the smoke signals to two Texas restaurants. At Bin Tapas Bar in San Antonio, Texas, Roger and Chef Jason Dady cook a giant pan of paella flavored with Thai ingredients and cooked over a wood burning fire.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Meat Masters
programme.desc: Roger Mooking meets two barbecue brainiacs who have mastered the art of marrying heat and meat to turn out top-notch barbecue. Pitmaster Christopher Prieto teaches students the science of smoking and seasoning meats at Prime Barbecue.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Getting Piggy With It
programme.desc: Roger Mooking goes hog-wild at two legendary barbecue restaurants located in America's Barbecue Belt. At A&R Bar-B-Que in Memphis, Roger helps owner Andrew Pillard load racks of St. Louis-style ribs into custom wood-fired pits.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Alaska
programme.desc: Casey's nationwide feast brings him to Alaska, where he cracks into crab legs fit for a king at Slippery Salmon Bar and Grill in Anchorage. In Talkeetna, he samples the local wild game, ordering a slab of reindeer meatloaf at Denali BrewPub.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Jose, California
programme.desc: Casey Webb's epicurean expedition takes him to San Jose, CA. His first stop is a one-of-a-kind drive-in for a crispy and golden falafel sandwich.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation: Boston, MA
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to historic Boston, Massachusetts, for some epic New England eats and a 4.5-pound Irish breakfast challenge at Flann O'Brien's Pub.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Daytona Beach, Florida
programme.desc: Casey's delicious mission brings him to Daytona Beach, Florida, where he takes on a mammoth three-tier sandwich stacked with two burgers, six pieces of Mahi Mahi, two pieces of Texas toast and a ten-ounce bun.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Sizzling Southwest
programme.desc: Enchiladas, burritos, fajitas, ole! We're counting them down one by one, culminating in a Tex Mex Treat so stupendous, it's coming in at number one on the countdown.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Grill Masters
programme.desc: Armed with a serious love of food and years of experience in the restaurant business, Casey Webb travels the country in search of America's most delicious dishes and ultimate eating challenges.

programme.title: Eddie Eats America: Manhattan
programme.desc: Stoke strongman Eddie Hall goes head to head against long-time rival Brian Shaw, pulling a 23-ton truck and attempting the notorious skyscraper 'Monster' sandwich from Sarge's Deli.

programme.title: Eddie Eats America: New Jersey
programme.desc: Eddie Hall is in the Garden State of New Jersey for the Destination Dogs hot dog challenge. The epic platter pits Eddie against eight supersized hot dogs weighing in at nearly 6 pounds and a jaw-dropping 7,000 calories.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Supremely Smokey and Dizzyingly Delicious
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Savannah, where fried chicken and pancake tacos are a delicious reinvention of classic chicken and waffles. In Seattle, a burger with a house-made chorizo patty brings serious Spanish flavor to the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Triple Meat Trifecta
programme.desc: In Newport, R.I., Michael Symon dives into a triple meat-stacked sandwich with smoked pork belly, bologna and chicken liver pate. A decadent, ultra-crispy and deep-fried soft-shell crab patty in San Francisco will change the way you look at burgers.

programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to historic Boston, Massachusetts, for some epic New England eats and a 4.5-pound Irish breakfast challenge at Flann O'Brien's Pub.

programme.title: Seaside Snacks and Shacks: From Maine to Miami
programme.desc: Sabin Lomac heads to Manach, La., to try the thin-fried catfish, hush puppies and alligator sliders that draw crowds all the way from New Orleans. Then, he visits an artisan sausage joint in Traverse City, Mich.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Grilled and Que'd
programme.desc: Nothing goes better with beachin' than a little grillin' and que'in'. The dishes are diverse but they all boast that amazing flavor and char that fire brings to the table. In Arizona, Katie Lee joins a local chef to grill up a striper.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Capital City Cuisine
programme.desc: Ali Khan, full-blown food enthusiast and blogger, has 12 hours and only 35 bucks to find the best deals in Washington DC for breakfast, lunch, dinner and a snack.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Old Town Grub
programme.desc: Ali Khan, full-blown food enthusiast and blogger, has 12 hours and only 35 bucks to find the best deals in Alexandria, VA: for breakfast, lunch, dinner and a snack! Ali kicks things off in style with a guilt free southern fried chicken biscuit.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Chowin' Down In Portland
programme.desc: Food blogger, Ali Khan, has 12 hours and only 35 bucks to find the best deals in Portland.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Stuffed In Spokane
programme.desc: Ali breaks his fast with a farm-to-food truck twist on the classic P.B.& J in Spokane.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations: El Bulli
programme.desc: On the coast of Spain, Tony has the experience of a lifetime while cooking and dining with Chef Ferran Adria.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: North Carolina's Triangle
programme.desc: Andrew digs into Raleigh, Durham and Chapel Hill- three tasty cities that make up North Carolina's Triangle. A hub for higher education with a booming tech industry, this region is home to a blossoming food scene.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Dallas
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern explores the big and bold flavors that are decidedly Dallas. From comforting chicken fried steak and sweet, creamy pecan pie to hearty tortilla soup and steak aged for 240 days.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Washington, D.C.
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern looks at the diverse and monumental eats that define Washington, DC. Full of vibrant communities, DC boasts gastronomy that includes local oysters, Ethiopian chicken stew, spicy Chinese stir-fry and chilli-smothered sausages.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Cape Town
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern leaves no meal unexplored in multicultural Cape Town, South Africa, where meat comes in all forms, from sausage to stew to pie. The coastal city's seafood is out of this world, and the favorite pastry is nothing without a coat of syrup!

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Hot and Spicy
programme.desc: Things start heating up when Tyler Florence, Ted Allen, Ina Garten, The Neelys and others share their best ever hot and spicy dishes.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Guilty Pleasures
programme.desc: On the menu is a 24 layer chocolate cake and succulent duck smothered in gravy and cheese.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Steak Out
programme.desc: We're paying tribute to the best styles, cuts and steakhouses serving up the best steaks. Chef Jonathon Sawyer takes us on a trip to Dayton for a ribeye cut so incredibly, it's fit for a king.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: The Upper Crust
programme.desc: The best part of any dish is what holds it all together: the crust! Chef Beau MacMillan's favorite pot pie in Phoenix, Arizona, takes on Duff Goldman's seafood pie pick in Baton Rouge, Louisiana.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: The Closers
programme.desc: There's no better way to end a meal than with these delicious dishes. In New York City, Martha Stewart gushes over the most irresistible piece of lemon meringue she's ever had.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Let's Do Lunch
programme.desc: Ali Khan makes a beeline for an over-the-top pistachio chicken salad creation in Austin, Texas.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Smoked and Stacked
programme.desc: Duff Goldman makes a guest appearance at Michael Symon's favorite Baton Rouge, La. smokehouse that boasts a pork belly hoagie so outrageous it has to be shared. Next up in St. Louis, a big fat Greek burger ignites Michael's cultural soul, and in Austin.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Sweet and Savory
programme.desc: Scott Conant shows Michael his favourite burger and brew bar in an out-of-the-way corner of New Orleans, and then in Kansas City, Michael learns how two brother have stormed the classic barbecue scene with their award winning smoked meat.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls: And the Pearl Necklace
